name: Vegetable Shtrudel (Sephardic Recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Stew the vegetables in a tightly covered pot using only the water that clings to them.
2. When tender, season with salt and pepper to taste and add 1 tablespoon oil.
3. Mix together the water and flour until smooth and stir in lightly, shaking the pot to prevent sticking.
4. Cook over low heat for 3 to 4 minutes longer.
5. Let cool before spreading evenly on thin sheets of shtrudel pastry dough.
6. Roll up, brush with remaining oil, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
7. Bake at 375°F for 20 minutes or until nicely browned.
8. Cut the shtrudel rolls diagonally into 2 1/2- or 3-inch slices while warm, before removing from the baking pan or cookie sheet.
9. Serve with fish or dairy meals. Alternatively, serve with meat or poultry if prepared with oil or vegetable shortening.
